pi_items.source: Python pandas script mimicking tidytext sentiment analysis on Kaggle dataset

AFINN-111 lexicon search revealed that most target words are entirely absent from the sentiment lexicon, meaning they cannot mathematically contribute to negative sentiment scores. Only 'disappointed' is found in the top 20 list.

privileged_info_summary: I audited the task and discovered it is completely defective for two critical reasons. First, the data URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freestack/womens-ecommerce-clothing-reviews/master/Womens%20Clothing%20E-Commerce%20Reviews.csv' in setup_task.sh returns a 404, causing the environment setup to permanently fail. Second, the evaluation logic is fatally flawed: the task explicitly requires the agent to calculate the top 20 negative words using the AFINN lexicon, but the verifier mandates that the output contains at least 2 of 10 specified 'negative_keywords' (e.g., 'small', 'tight', 'fabric'). Because most of these words are completely absent from the AFINN lexicon, and only 'disappointed' mathematically appears in the true top 20 AFINN list for the Dresses class, an ideal solution yields exactly 1 keyword match, perpetually failing the verifier's 'found >= 2' condition.
